generated_comment: Arre bhai, these days in big companies half the promotions are for optics also. That lady VP may be smart, I am not denying, but people don't rise that fast on merit alone — quota, visibility, some senior backing, all that matters. Aur waise bhi, to survive at that level a woman has to become almost like a man only, otherwise nobody takes her seriously.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines a woman's promotion as not fully merit-based and frames leadership competence as requiring her to become 'like a man.' It encodes merit-undermining, quota suspicion, senior-relationship/speculation, and masculinization as a backhanded compliment.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
